data.title: Dow, LyondellBasell cut at JPM as U.S. exports more polyethylene to China than any other country
data.description:
Dow (NYSE:DOW) and LyondellBasell (NYSE:LYB) -9.7% and -7.6%, respectively, in Friday's trading as J.P. Morgan downgrades the petrochem companies to Hold from Buy with respective $31 and $60 price targets, lowered from $40 and $80, saying "petrochemical demand is faltering, raw material costs are rising, trade risk is increasing, [and] global economic strength is weakening."

Dow (NYSE:DOW) is "in the most difficult position of the petrochemical companies," JPM's Jeffrey Zekauskas says, as weak demand in China and Europe, global capacity additions in each of the company's three business segments, higher interest rates, and a slow global  economy have eroded its EBITDA.

Dow (NYSE:DOW) offers an 8.9% dividend yield, a cyclically low earnings profile, and leverage to lower interest rates, but its value is sensitive to changes in global economic activity, and recessionary risk is rising, meaning the company's EBITDA along with its share price can fall sharply under recessionary conditions, the analyst writes, adding that each $10/bbl change in the Brent crude price probably impacts the company's annual EBITDA by ~$1B.

Zekauskas also is concerned that the market may view Dow's (DOW) hefty dividend yield as unsustainable given the volatility and uncertainty in quarterly EBITDA.

LyondellBasell (NYSE:LYB) is "a large, efficient commodity petrochemical company," Zekauskas says, but it is subject to many of the same stresses as Dow (DOW) in the context of declining market sentiment, rising recessionary risk and greater economic uncertainty.

Like Dow (DOW), LyondellBasell's (LYB) EBITDA can fall sharply under recessionary conditions in part due to the decline in oil prices, the analyst says, estimating each $10/bbl change in the Brent price probably affects the company's annual EBITDA by ~$400M.

data.title: Enterprise Bancorp shareholders approve merger into Independent Bank
data.description:
• Bank holding company Enterprise Bancorp (NASDAQ:EBTC) said on Friday that its shareholders have approved the proposed merger into Independent Bank (NASDAQ:INDB).
  • The special meeting of shareholders was held virtually on April 3.
  • The two companies signed a definitive merger agreement in December last year, with the cash and stock transaction involving a consideration valued at ~$562M, or $45.06 per share.

data.title: IGM Financial reports marginally lower AUM and advisement in March
data.description:
• Canada-based IGM Financial (TSX:IGM:CA) announced on Friday that its March 2025 AUM and advisement stood at $275.0B.
  • The figure compares with $278.2B in February, and $252.2B in March 31, 2024.
  • The asset manager reported net inflows of $3.3B for March 2025.

data.title: Ams outlines growth strategy with new Rhode Island and Mexico facilities
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: American Shared Hospital Services (AMS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Executive Chairman Ray Stachowiak announced the appointment of Gary Delanois as the new CEO. He emphasized the company's strategic transition towards patient-centric services and highlighted their strong revenue growth driven by acquisitions and new facility openings.
  • Revenue for Q4 2024 increased 59% year-over-year, supported by the Rhode Island acquisition and the opening of a new radiation therapy treatment facility in Puebla, Mexico. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter rose 29% compared to the same period in 2023.
  • CEO Gary Delanois detailed strategic investments in Rhode Island, including equipment upgrades and staffing optimization. He also highlighted the operational improvements achieved through the partnership with Brown University Health System for radiation oncologist staffing.
  • Delanois outlined the company's expansion into international markets with facilities in Peru, Ecuador, and new centers in Mexico, including a joint venture in Guadalajara.
  • CFO Scott Frech reported fiscal year 2024 revenue of $28.34 million, a 33% increase from 2023, with significant growth in the direct patient services segment. The company experienced a decline in the equipment leasing segment due to contract expirations and reduced Gamma Knife volumes.

Outlook

  • Management expects continued growth from the Rhode Island centers, supported by strategic partnerships and operational enhancements.
  • The company anticipates stronger international growth from increased treatment capabilities in Ecuador, expanded volumes in Peru, and operations from the new centers in Puebla and Guadalajara, Mexico.
  • Future growth opportunities include the development of a fourth radiation therapy center and a proton beam radiation therapy center in Rhode Island, both of which received Certificate of Need approvals.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenue was reported at $9.1 million, an increase of 59% compared to Q4 2023, driven by the Rhode Island acquisition and Puebla facility.
  • The direct patient services segment showed a 420% increase in revenue for Q4 2024, contrasting with a decline in the equipment leasing segment.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2024 was $3.5 million, up from $2.7 million in Q4 2023.
  • Net loss for Q4 2024 was $1.3 million, compared to a net income of $415,000 in Q4 2023. The loss was influenced by increased reserves for impaired assets and removal costs.

Q&A

  • Analyst from Zacks inquired about benefits from the Rhode Island expansion and potential synergies. Stachowiak highlighted collaborative relationships with major healthcare providers in the state and strategic operational efficiencies.
  • An Eastwood Partners analyst questioned the impact of outdated equipment on referrals and the strategy for Gamma Knife performance. Stachowiak explained that equipment upgrades have restored referral volumes and outlined efforts to enhance community awareness of Gamma Knife centers.
  • A private investor expressed concerns about stock performance and transparency. Stachowiak emphasized the company's long-term strategic goals, geographic and product diversification, and the transition to patient services.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns over operational challenges and stock performance but acknowledged the growth potential of recent acquisitions and expansions.
  • Management maintained a confident tone regarding strategic initiatives and long-term growth, but demonstrated defensiveness when addressing current financial setbacks.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone reflected stronger confidence in the Rhode Island and international expansions but remained cautious about near-term challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 revenue growth of 59% exceeded Q3's 36% year-over-year growth rate, driven by new facilities and acquisitions.
  • Adjusted EBITDA showed improvement from $1.37 million in Q3 to $3.5 million in Q4.
  • Management's tone shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 to a more confident outlook on strategic initiatives in Q4, particularly regarding Rhode Island and international operations.
  • Analysts' focus remained consistent on challenges with Gamma Knife volumes and operational efficiencies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified challenges with integrating acquired facilities, including the need for equipment upgrades and staffing transitions.
  • Analysts raised concerns about Gamma Knife volume declines and the financial impact of contract expirations.
  • Management detailed mitigation strategies, including partnerships with healthcare providers, community awareness campaigns, and operational improvements.

Final Takeaway

American Shared Hospital Services emphasized its strategic pivot towards patient-centric services, supported by significant growth in direct patient care and international expansion. The new facilities in Rhode Island and Mexico, alongside operational upgrades, are expected to drive future performance. Despite current challenges in the leasing segment and Gamma Knife volumes, management remains confident in its long-term growth prospects and strategic direction.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Healthcare firms, Hinge, Medline are reportedly delaying IPOs amid market rout
data.description:
Digital physical therapy startup Hinge Health (HNGE) and medical supply firm Medline Industries are reportedly delaying their IPO plans in response to the market selloff driven by President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariff measures.

According to Business Insider, Hinge Health (HNGE), which filed for its long-awaited New York IPO last month, is looking to delay its offering as tariff concerns weigh on the U.S. markets.

The San Francisco, California-based firm had previously planned to launch its IPO in April, CNBC reported last month, citing a person familiar with the matter. Despite the delay, the company intends to stick to its spring IPO timeline,  Business Insider reported on Friday.

One of the largest manufacturers and distributors of medical supplies, Medline, has delayed its IPO plans indefinitely, The Financial Times reported on Friday. Medline, backed by private equity firms Blackstone (BX) and Carlyle (CG), was expected to announce its IPO filing earlier this week for a valuation of nearly $50B.

Additionally, Swedish fintech firm Klarna (KLAR) and ticketing marketplace StubHub (STUB) have reportedly delayed their much-awaited IPOs amid the market selloff.

data.title: Associated Capital updates on Q1 book value
data.description:
• Associated Capital Group (NYSE:AC) announced on Friday a preliminary range for its Q1 book value of $42.42-$42.62 per share.
  • The book value as of December 31, 2024, stood at $42.14.
  • The investment advisory services provider's shares were trading 9.32% down Friday late afternoon at $32.20.
  • The company is set to report its financial results in May.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Tariff drama, big banks report earnings, Walmart meeting, and the CPI print
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Saturday - April 5

  • All day - The OPEC+ Joint Ministerial Monitoring Committee will convene online to review the supply decision for May.

 

Monday - April 7

  • Volatility watch - Super Micro Computer (SMCI) and Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) have a high level of implied volatility based on options trading. Semler Scientific (SMLR) and Quantum Corporation (QMCO) have an elevated level of short interest outstanding on them heading into the new week. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Maywood Acquisition (MAYA), ProAssurance (PRA), and Radius Recycling (RDUS). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Xerox (XRX) and Vaxcyte (PCVX). Newsmax (NMAX) will also be watched closely after a wild first week of trading.
  • IPO watch - Network software firm Fatpipe (FATN) is expected to price its IPO and start trading. Blocks of shares of KinderCare (KLC), Click Holdings (CLIK), and Upstream Bio (UPB) move past their IPO lockup period.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Levi Strauss (LEVI) and Dave & Buster's (PLAY).
  • All day - The NAB Show in Las Vegas will continue after starting over the weekend. Comcast (CMCSA), Verizon (VZ), and Sinclair (SBGI) are some of the companies expected to make announcements.
  • All day - Speakers at the TED Conference include OpenAI's Sam Altman, former Google (GOOG) CEO Eric Schmidt, and Whole Foods Market founder John Mackey.
  • All day - The four-day Fortinet (FTNT) Accelerate25 event will begin. CEO Ken Xie will be one of the keynote speakers. Shares of Fortinet (FTNT) have rallied in the past following the event.
  • All day - The four-day Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference will include presentations and fireside chat from leading public and private companies in the Biopharma, Medtech, Diagnostics and Digital Health sectors. Participating companies include Immunome (IMNM), Lucid Diagnostics (LUCD), Rocket Pharmaceuticals (RCKT), and Alkermes (ALKS).
  • All day - The four-day Space Symposium conference in Colorado Springs will feature representatives from the U.S. Space Force, Palantir (PLTR), NASA, Amazon (AMZN) AWS, Northrop Grumman (NOC), Raytheon (RTX), L3Harris (LHX), Blue Origin (BORGN), and SpaceX (SPACE).

 

Tuesday - April 8

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA), Tilray (TLRY), and Cal-Maine Foods (CALM).
  • All day - The two-day Stifel Virtual Targeted Oncology Forum will include participation from Compugen (CGEN), Zymeworks (ZYME), Innate Pharma (IPHA), and Revolution Medicines (RVMD).
  • All day - Atlassian (TEAM) will hold its three-day Team25 event in Anaheim, California. CEO Mike Cannon-Brookes will be one of the keynote speakers.
  • All day - Paris Blockchain Week will begin. Featured speakers include Yves Choueifaty of TOBAM, Anthony Scaramucci of Skybridge, and Jean-Marie Mognetti of CoinShares.
  • All day - The EIA will publish its short-term energy outlook.

 

Wednesday - April 9

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Constellation Brands (STZ), PriceSmart (PSMT), and Delta Air Lines (DAL). 
  • All day - New country-specific tariffs mandated by the U.S. will become effective.
  • All day - The three-day Google Cloud Next event will begin in Las Vegas. Google Cloud and industry leaders are expected to make major new announcements and offer key perspectives.
  • 9:00 a.m. Walmart (WMT) will hold an investment community meeting.
  • 12:30 p.m. Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will participate in a conversation with the Economic Club of Washington.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release the minutes of its last meeting. Analysts think the detailed insights into the discussions and considerations of the policymakers could be more intriguing than normal after the Fed leaned into stagflation risks at the March FOMC meeting.
  • 4:00 p.m. Nasdaq will release its short interest data report.

 

Thursday - April 10

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include CarMax (KMX). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Lovesac (LOVE) after its report.
  • All day - Walmart (WMT) will hold its annual shareholder meeting.
  • All day - TD Synnex (SNX) will hold its Investor Day event.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Consumer Price Index report for March will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.
  • 12:00 p.m. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before the Economic Club of New York.
  • 2:30 p.m. Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook will speak on the economic outlook before the University of Pittsburgh 2025 McKay Lecture event.

 

Friday - April 11

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), Morgan Stanley (MS), Blackrock (BLK), and Bank of New York Mellon (BK). Options trading implies a double-digit swing when Children's Place (PLCE) reports.
  • 8:30 a.m. The producer price index report for March will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.
  • 11:00 a.m. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will speak on the economic outlook and monetary policy before the Puerto Rico Chamber of Commerce.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: Dow hits correction, Nasdaq in bear-market territory as Trump tariffs spur growth fears
data.description:
U.S. stocks fell sharply Friday, losing further ground after Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell pointed to monetary-policy uncertainty in the face of fiscal policies from the Trump administration. 

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) pushed into bear-market territory and the Dow (DJI) dropped into a correction as investors assess sweeping new U.S. import tariffs that President Donald Trump announced this week. China, in retaliation, has slapped a 34% levy on U.S. products. 

"Turning to monetary policy, we face a highly uncertain outlook with elevated risks of both higher unemployment and higher inflation," Powell said in a late-Friday morning speech in Virginia. "The new Administration is in the process of implementing substantial policy changes in four distinct areas: trade, immigration, fiscal policy, and regulation," Powell said.

But "the economy is still in a good place," while uncertainty is high and downside risks have risen, Powell said.

In early afternoon trade, the S&P 500 (SP500) was -5.4% at 5,101.25. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -5.5%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) -5.1%, losing +2,000 points, at 38,477.83.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was down ~17% from its all-time closing high in mid-February, and the Dow (DJI) was off ~14% from its record closing high in December. On Friday, the Energy sector (XLE) -7.6%, leading all 11 groups lower on the market benchmark (SP500). 

The bull market is "dead" as Trump's tariffs could "devastate the global economy," Bowersock Capital Partners CEO Emily Bowersock Hill said as the firm cut its 2025 S&P 500 (SP500) target to 5,700 from 6,000. 

The S&P 500(SP500) closed in a correction Thursday, and fell 4.8% for its worst session since June 2020. The index was on track for its worst week since a -14.98% weekly decline in March 2020. 

In the bond market Friday, U.S. Treasury yields slumped as traders flocked to the safety of bonds. But the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) trimmed its loss, falling 5 basis points to 4.01%, and the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 8 basis points to 3.65%.

Market participants did receive a reprieve on Friday in the form of a surprisingly strong March jobs report. Nonfarm payrolls rose 228K, blowing past expectations. The unemployment rate edged up to 4.2%. 

"Friday's jobs report shows that the economy is still creating jobs at a robust pace even with the tariff uncertainty and Federal job cuts, and job growth is arguably the most important economic indicator," Glen Smith, chief investment officer at GDS Wealth Management, said.

However, the data are "backward looking and doesn't say anything about how employers might fare over the coming months," Smith said. 

RBC Capital Markets on Friday cut its 2025 S&P 500 (SP500) target by 11%, to 5,500, as it sees growth-scare risks rising amid Trump's tariff plans. 

Friday's session did see a rebound in shares of Nike (NKE), Gap (GAP), and other retailers after Vietnam and Cambodia expressed willingness to negotiate tariff levels. 

Striking an optimistic tone, Wall Street veteran Edward Yardeni told Bloomberg TV said he sees "great buying opportunities" emerging within the tariff-fueled crash in stocks.

data.title: Microsoft touts AI Copilot features on 50th birth anniversary
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) celebrated its 50th birthday on Friday with a special Copilot Event where it unveiled its latest AI developments and reminisced its rich history.

The Redmond, Wash.-based company announced features to its AI product Copilot at the event celebrating its golden anniversary. 

“Today is not just about the past 50 years but the next 50” said CEO Satya Nadella.

He used Copilot Agent mode to help perform a task, similar to what the company's first product did 50 years ago.

Earlier this week, Microsoft Co-Founder Bill Gates had shared memories from his past, including the original source code to Altair Basic, which would become the first product of the company. "That code remains the coolest code I've ever written to this day," said Gates in a blog post on April 2. It was a programming language interpreter for the MITS Altair 8800, which was created by an electronics company called MITS.

The new capability with Agent Mode will be rolled out to all VS users from today, according to Nadella. He added that the company is also bringing code review agent to fix and find bugs automatically. 

The Microsoft chief added that the company is making it easier for developers to build their own agents in foundry.

Nadella noted that they now have a new agent framework for building multi-agent systems. The company is also building tools for all the evaluations, fine-tuning, observability and feedback.

Nadella said the company will also "have an AI Red Teaming Agent and tools to measure code vulnerability, all of this in support of building trustworthy AI systems."

"What started out as a developer tools company 50 years ago is now a platform company where everyone can be a developer," Nadella commented. 

Microsoft showed examples of people who are using Copilot for help in their lives. 

Mustafa Suleyman, CEO of Microsoft AI, introduced some Copilot features such as Copilot Pages, Copilot Actions, Windows Vision and Copilot Deep Research.

Copilot Pages helps users write, for example, a letter to a landlord. Copilot Actions can be used, for example, to help find listings of apartments.

Through Windows Vision, Copilot, with permission from users, can help edit photos on screen. Copilot Deep Research can help users plan trips.

Microsoft showed Copilot's new search capabilities. The tool can show picture cards, which are backed by weblinks.

Copilot will have Memory, and it will be able to offer a graphical representation of information it knows about you. 

Microsoft is also experimenting with different appearances for Copilot, such an abstract or fun animation character, like Clippy.

Suleyman also touted a podcast recorded by Copilot with Nadella and former CEOs Bill Gates and Steve Ballmer. Suleyman also showcased a podcast created by the AI tool on dinosaurs for a child’s birthday party.

Chiefs of several tech companies, including AMD (AMD), Adobe (ADBE) and Accenture (ACN) sent out wishes to the software giant, acknowledging the work done by the company over the years.

“I’m incredibly proud of our collaboration and joint work together over the years—from driving the industry standard for personal computing with Windows, to powering the most important Azure cloud services used by so many every day with AMD EPYC and Instinct, to reaching billions of gamers with Xbox,” said AMD’s Chair and CEO Lisa Su.

data.title: Retailers rebound as Vietnam and Cambodia express willingness to negotiate
data.description:
Thursday's steep sell-off in the retail sector tied to tariffs on Southeast Asian imports are staging a significant rebound after President Trump’s ‘very productive call’ with General Secretary To Lam of Vietnam and Cambodian prime minister Hun Manet. 

Accordingly, shares of Nike (NYSE:NKE), Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU), Gap (NYSE:GAP), Skechers (NYSE:SKX) On Holdings (NYSE:ONON), Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF), Victoria's Secret (NYSE:VSCO), Guess? (NYSE:GES), and Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN) have reversed all or most of their losses on Thursday and are 4% to 14% in the green.

During the call, General Secretary To told the president he would like to cut their tariffs (on U.S. imports) “down to ZERO if they are able to make an agreement with the U.S.”

Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet, who also spoke with Trump, expressed a similar willingness to lower tariffs on imported goods from the U.S. in return for equal accommodation. 

On Thursday, shares of those companies that manufacture a majority of their merchandise in Southeast Asian countries like Vietnam, Cambodia, and Sri Lanka suffered heavy losses after tariffs ranging from 30% to 49% were announced.

While Friday's price action helped trimmed some of the sector's weekly loss, shares are set to close in the red at the end of a very tough week for the retail sector.

In late Friday trading, Nike (NYSE:NKE), On Holdings (NYSE:ONON), Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF) were all set to close in the red for a fifth straight week, down another ~7%, ~8%, and ~4%, respectively. Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) and Victoria's Secret (NYSE:VSCO) are lower for a second week with a loss of ~9%,and ~7%, respectively. Skechers (NYSE:SKX) is down 10% on the week, while Guess? (NYSE:GES) was the outlier, closing in the green this week as Q4 results helped offset tariff-related losses.
